en.extract:
The Makira cicadabird (Edolisoma salomonis) is a species of bird in the family Campephagidae.
It is endemic to the Solomon Islands.  It used to be considered a subspecies of the common cicadabird.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

The '''Makira cicadabird''' ('''''Edolisoma salomonis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Campephagidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Solomon Islands]].  It used to be considered a subspecies of the [[common cicadabird]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].
